observations.recommendation:
2.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer, and the Sangguniang Bayan to
facilitate the withdrawal of the amount of ₱5,589,996.92 under DBP
0725-020643-530 and re-appropriate the funds for other purposes
essential to the operations of the LGU’s mission and objectives.

3.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary documents required for the filing
of writing off of the account balance of ₱1,550,603.33 to cleanse the
accounts in order to come up with a fair presentation of the Due to
NGAs account in the financial statements.

4.10. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to locate source documents and
Memorandum of Agreement with the agency to review the terms and
conditions corresponding to the fund transfer in relation to the
implementation of programs and projects. Prepare and maintain a
subsidiary ledger for the account to reflect transactions affecting
thereto;
b) Require project officers/focal persons to immediately submit
liquidation reports for the said account; and
c) Designate a focal person who would be responsible in monitoring the
status of project implementation of funds transferred to NGOs/POs.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer reported to:
a) Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks. A replacement check may be issued upon submission of the stale check;
b) Practice sending written notice to the payee at least one prior month before a check becomes stale; and
c) Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the necessary General Journal adjustment/s in the books of accounts, if any.
observations.managementAction: Written notice had been sent to the respective payees.
The necessary adjustments had been made and reflected in the revised financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the MENRO or the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to submit a report of all donations officially received by its office.

Henceforth, observe proper documentation and reporting process in the receipt and issuance of the In-Kind Donations received by the LGU and ensure that the report is sufficient in detail to facilitate proper recording and accounting.
observations.managementAction: Not Accounting Office has not received any documents from MENRO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary accounting entries to reclassify the completed projects to their appropriate asset/expense accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments for CIP is reflected in the January 2024 General Journal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend for the Local Chief Executive require:
a) the Municipal Accountant to: i) ensure provision of allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets procured by the LGU until such item is fully depreciated and/or derecognized; and, ii) properly reflect in the notes to financial statements the PPE valuation and depreciations schedules of all the PPE items booked under all funds; and
b) Enjoin the Inventory Committee to include all PPE items in the physical count and require the GSO/Municipal Treasurer that property records be updated based on the result thereof, and reconcile with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the LGU’s PPE.
observations.managementAction: All depreciable PPE items are provided with an allowance for depreciation. The MTO/GSO records regarding PPEs are updated and reconciled with the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to classify the balances of the Loan Payable-Domestic Account as Current Liability or Non-Current Liability, as prescribed under Paragraphs 70 and 80 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: No amortization schedule was furnished by the MDFO. Hence, the amount was entirely classified as non-current for the meantime.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting and/or closing entries pertaining to balances of projects that have already been liquidated and/or closing entries and institute appropriate remedies to clear the unsubstantiated/undocumented amount of ₱5,139.38 in the books to ensure the reliability of the balance reported in the Due to NGAs account.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated fund transfers are already adjusted in the books and the pertinent adjustments/closing entries had been reflected in the revised financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct:

a) The Municipal Planning and Development Council to secure a copy of the Feasibility Study submitted to MDFO, review its viability taking into consideration the current location of the LEE and come up with the 5-Year Business Plan and strategize to effectively put into use the infrastructure projects in the LIETCO and generate economic benefits to both the LGU and the public in general; and

b) The Municipal Engineering Office to expedite the completion of the infrastructure projects to fast track the realization of economic benefits for the LGU and the public.
observations.managementAction: The MTO/Municipal Engineer is not working on the provision of a Comprehensive Development Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

SB members and concerned offices to formulate well-defined policy guidelines on the grant of assistance to come up with an updated resolution in consonance with the guidelines issued by the DSWD; and

MSWDO to submit the MATRIX for the AICS program prepared and approved by the Municipal Mayor to the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan for proper disposition.
observations.managementAction: The necessary supporting documents are attached. The approved copy of the matrix is still not prepared and submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct:

a) The Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursements are duly supported with complete and proper documents;

b) The personnel concerned to liquidate the cash advances granted for their operational activities by submitting the activities accomplishment reports and other supporting documents to prove that such was utilized in accordance with the intended purpose; and

c) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from signing claims unless authorized.
observations.managementAction: Expenses of the NGAs are not accommodated, as far as known, and a portion of the necessary activities is already reflected in the revised financial statements. The SB and Mayor’s Office require liquidation as proof of grants.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to optimize the use of its DRRM funds taking into consideration all the thematic areas as mandated by Republic Act No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: A portion of the 70% Mitigation Fund is allocated for Capital Outlay to ensure that basic rescue and response equipment are procured. The LGU ensures full coordination with affected communities and organizations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the duly created Disposal and Appraisal Committee to expedite the process of the disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: A list of unserviceable properties has been prepared by the General Services Office (GSO). The Disposal and Appraisal Committee (DAC) must be reconstituted. In addition, the DAC is expected to convene and revisit the newly issued guidelines on the disposal of government properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the causes of the negative balances in the subsidiary accounts, keep track of its records for proper disclosure and prepare an adjusting entry to bring the balances to its correct amount, henceforth establish with certainty the obligations to provide a reliable information in the financial statements pursuant to Item no. 29 of PPSAS 1, Volume 1 of PPSAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments and the same had been reflected in the revised financial statements.
Project implementation is based on project designs and estimates as the preparation of variation orders is minimized. Relocation site for affected communities has been procured by the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Instruct the Planning In-Charge to judiciously plan and evaluate future proposals to ensure that the project would be beneficial to the constituents and could be used after completion; comply with the required detailed engineering activities for all infrastructure project included in the Annual Investment Plan;

b) Require the Project Monitoring Committee to inform immediately the contractor of the defects discovered and require him to undertake repair or forfeit the performance security, when applicable; and

c) Assign an employee to properly maintain the property.
observations.managementAction: The project has been turned over to the respective barangays as of November 21, 2022. The Barangay will be in charge of maintenance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the LDRRM Officer to review the PPAs embodied in the LDRRM Investment Plan and ensure that it addresses the mandated four thematic areas, and strictly utilize and/or implement them with the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact as required under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The LGU ensures full coordination with other concerned departments. Continuous monitoring of the plan and utilization reports is being conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend for the Local Chief Executive to require:
a) the Municipal Engineer to provide the complete description and cost segregation for road projects; and
b) the Municipal Accountant to maintain the General Ledger, Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, Working Paper on the cost distribution for road components, subsidiary records for road components, depreciation schedule for the depreciable component of the network system, and prepare the Notes to the Financial Statements as embodied in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer provided the complete description and cost segregation component for road projects. The accounting office maintains the general ledger and depreciation schedule and provided a copy of the ledger as of May 2024 to the engineering office for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Accounting Office to maintain the necessary SLs for a particular controlling account to facilitate verification of the transactions and in compliance with the above cited circulars.

Likewise, update from time to time the SLs and reconcile the same to the corresponding controlling account in order to rectify errors, omission, if any, at an earlier date before the financial statements shall be prepared.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjustments are accordingly. The accounting office considered the advice of the auditor to properly disclose the amounts that cannot be identified.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

Require the designated Supply Officer and the Disposal Committee to prepare the corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) for the identified unserviceable properties; and

After the disposal, require the Supply Officer to submit the necessary documents, together with the official receipt to the Accounting Office, as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: A list of unserviceable properties has already been prepared; however, the DAC still needs to revisit the guidelines on the disposal of these properties. The reconstitution of the Disposal and Appraisal Committee (DAC) is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the MSWD Officer prepare the summary/list and reports and furnish the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular 2014-002 and 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: The report and summary/list were prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our prior years’ recommendation that Management expedite the implementation of the programs/projects in order to serve the purpose for which the funds were released.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 projects funded from various NGAs are fully implemented and liquidated. The necessary adjustments are reflected in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all parcels of land owned by the Municipality be registered under the name of the Municipality to protect such properties from third party claims.
observations.managementAction: The titling of some land is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-Agency accounts, “Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds” to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances and effect the necessary adjustments and, if appropriate, to bring the affected accounts into an agreement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments and the same had been reflected in the revised financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to:

a) Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale
checks. A replacement check may be issued upon submission of the
stale check;

b) Practice sending written notice to the payee at least one month before
a check becomes stale; and

c) Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the necessary
adjustment/s in the books of accounts, if any.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant the following courses of action:

a) Conduct periodic monitoring, analysis and reconciliation of the
funds transferred between the books of the LGU and the recipient
agencies, as well as those whose whereabouts could not be
determined;

b) Verify and validate the dormant accounts to any available source
documents and draw the necessary adjusting entry if it was a result
of an accounting error, or if warranted, include in the request for
write-off;

c) Conduct a comprehensive review, analysis and evaluation of the
inactive and non-moving accounts and subsequently submit request
for write-off in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016; and

d) Analyze discrepancies noted from confirmations with the IAs and
reconcile/adjust accordingly. Also, make representation with the IAs
concerned to investigate, verify and reconcile the records on those
funds transferred but reported as not received by them and not
existing in their books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MENRO or
the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to submit a report of all donations
officially received by its office.

Henceforth, observe proper documentation and reporting process in the
receipt and issuance of the In-Kind Donations received by the LGU and
ensure that the report is sufficient in detail to facilitate proper recording
and accounting.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare necessary accounting entries to reclassify the
completed projects to their appropriate asset/expense accounts.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive the following courses of
action:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to: i) ensure provision of an
allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets procured by the
LGU until such item is fully depreciated and/or derecognized; and,
ii) properly reflect in the notes to financial statements the PPE
valuation and depreciations schedules of all the PPE items booked
under all funds; and

b) Enjoin the Inventory Committee to include all PPE items in the
physical count and the require the GSO/Municipal Treasurer that
property records be updated based on the result thereof, and
reconcile with accounting records to come up with the reconciled
balances of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to classify the balances of the Loan Payable-Domestic
Account as Current Liability or Non-Current Liability, as prescribed
under Paragraphs 70 and 80 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary adjusting and/or closing entries
pertaining to balances of projects that have already been liquidated and
institute appropriate remedies to clear the
unsubstantiated/undocumented amount of ₱5,139.38 in the books to
ensure the reliability of the balance reported in the Due to NGAs account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:

a) The Municipal Planning and Development Council to secure a copy
of the Feasibility Study submitted to MDFO, review its viability taking
into consideration the current location of the LEE and come up with the 5-
Year Business Plan and strategize to effectively put into use the
infrastructure projects in the LIETCO and generate economic benefits
to both the LGU and the public in general; and

b) The Municipal Engineering Office to expedite the completion of the
infrastructure projects to fast track the realization of economic
benefits for the LGU and the public.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

a) SB members and concerned offices to formulate well-defined policy
guidelines on the grant of assistance to come up with an updated
resolution in consonance with the guidelines issued by the DSWD;

b) MSWDO to maintain objectivity, in determining the type, kind and
amount of financial assistance to be recommended for approval of
the appropriate authority;

c) Municipal Accountant to ascertain the completeness of the required
supporting documents based on the guidelines; and

d) MSWDO to submit the MATRIX for the AICS program prepared
and approved by the Municipal Mayor to the Office of the
Sangguniang Bayan for proper disposition.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:

a) The Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursements are duly
supported with complete and proper documents;

b) The personnel concerned to liquidate the cash advances granted for
their operational activities by submitting the accomplishment
reports and other supporting documents to prove that such was
utilized in accordance with the intended purpose; and

c) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to
refrain from signing claims to accommodate expenses of the
personnel from NGAs unless duly authorized and with complete
documents to curb possible wastage of government funds.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of
Suspension or Notice of Disallowance whichever is appropriate in the
circumstances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:

a) The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to
optimize the use of its DRRM funds taking into consideration all the
thematic areas as mandated by Republic Act No. 10121; and

b) The Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds for the capital outlay
of the 70% Mitigation Fund to ensure that basic rescue and response
equipment are procured, hence, the municipality is more prepared
for any calamity as prescribed in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

1.13. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a) Inventory Committee to conduct complete annual physical inventory
count of the PPE using the guidelines enumerated in COA Circular
2020-006 and submit a copy of the RPCPPE/Inventory Report not
later than January 31 of each year using the prescribed format in
Annex A of the Circular;
b) The Municipal Treasurer/GSO and the Municipal Accountant to
update property records based on the procedural guidelines
provided in the same Circular to come up with the reconciled
balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of
the LGU’s PPE;
c) Municipal Budget Officer to create a plantilla position for General
Services Officer or a Property Officer who will competently
undertake the management and safekeeping of the LGUs properties; and
d) The duly created Disposal and Appraisal Committee to expedite the
process of the disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU.

2.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Municipal Department Heads to assign an employee within
its department who would prepare and submit the Requisition and
Issue Slip, monitor their supplies, and handle its safekeeping upon
issuance to their respective offices;
b) Relinquish some tasks from the designated Supply Officer in order
for her to devote full attention to the management of supplies and
inventories, prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and
51
Materials Issued (SSMI) and Report of Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI), pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual
for LGU; and
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to
reflect in the financial statements the correct value of the inventories
held by the LGU as of yearend.

3.13. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to trace the causes of the negative balances in the liability
accounts, keep track of its records for proper disclosure and prepare an
adjusting entry to bring the balances to its correct amount, henceforth
establish with certainty the obligations to provide a reliable information
in the financial statements pursuant to Item no. 29 of PPSAS 1, Volume
1 of PPSAS Manual.

4.9. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive the following courses of
action:
a) For the completed projects, direct the Municipal Accountant to make
demand letters to the concerned contractors to refund the amount;
b) Review the payments for the account with negative balances and
effect the necessary corrections/adjustments if any;
c) For suspended projects, resolve the issues with the Contractors
immediately and speed up the process of approving the revised plans and
specifications, change orders, and/or time suspension/extension;
d) For the projects which are for termination, demand the immediate
refund of the unrecouped advances or forfeit the corresponding
advance payment bonds posted by the contractors who were
declared in default; and
e) Require the Project Engineers concerned in coordination with the Project
Monitoring Committee to: (i) verify the projects with unrecouped
advances with no available data and which are over five years; (ii)
coordinate with contractors and other offices to deduct the amount from
any unpaid claims and from other projects undertaken by the same
contractors; and (iii) initiate legal action to those who refuse to refund
the unrecouped advances, if warranted.

5.6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV) to reclassify completed Infrastructure Projects from CIP
to the appropriate Infrastructure Asset account.

5.7. Further, we recommended coordination between the Municipal
Engineering Office and Municipal Accounting Office on the monitoring
of project status to timely make appropriate reclassification of completed
projects so as not to accumulate the amounts in the CIP account.

6.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Engineer and the Project Monitoring Committee the following courses
of action:
a) To adhere to the requirements prescribed under Section 17.6 of the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 regarding
the conduct of bidding and award for infrastructure contracts;
b) To resolve the issues such as RROW, relocation of informal settlers
and viability of the project sites be properly addressed during the
feasibility or preliminary engineering study prior to project
implementation. These problems should be disclosed during the
planning stage and extensively discussed when deliberations for the
project’s implementation are conducted with management officials
and implementing offices to avoid delay in the implementation of the
projects;
c) To thoroughly review and evaluate the Program of Work to ensure
that project designs and estimates are properly prepared and that
all phases of the projects are covered to minimize variation orders
and time extensions which often resulted in the increase of project
cost and delayed completion; and,
d) To ensure full coordination with other department heads concerned,
including the community and private utilities, to achieve effective
and more synchronized planning of programs and projects.

7.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Instruct the Planning In-Charge to judiciously plan and evaluate
future project proposals to ensure that the project would be
beneficial to the constituents and could be used after completion;
comply with the required detailed engineering activities for all
infrastructure project included in the Annual Investment Plan;
b) Require the Project Monitoring Committee to inform immediately
the contractor of the defects discovered and require him to
undertake repair or forfeit the performance security, when
applicable; and
c) Assign an employee to properly maintain the property.

8.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LDRRM
Officer to review the PPAs embodied in the LDRRM Investment Plan
and ensure that it addresses the mandated four thematic areas, and
strictly utilize and/or implement them with the highest priority in terms
of urgency and/or impact as required under Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

9.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, require the HRMO, and
the Local Finance Committee to:
a) Determine the necessity of hiring Casual and Job Order Employees,
and reasonableness of the rates, and assess their duties and
functions, with due considerations on their expertise, experience,
and capacity to handle the position in order to come up with a
recommendation as to the ideal number of employees to be hired;
b) Attach/Submit the duly certified copy of the Contracts/Service
Contracts of the Casual and Job Order with the statement of the
expected work or output; with the names of the
supervisor/timekeeper, the office where the employees should
report;
c) Submit duly filled and signed Daily Time Record and assigned time
keeper for those on the field to check on the attendance or services
rendered by the employees and be factual on the services rendered
by the employees; and
d) Assess its existing organizational structure so that the ideal number
of plantilla/casual as well as job order personnel will be established;
and hire only those that are necessary in the achievement of the
mission of the LGU.

10.10. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Health Officer to:
a) Provide a report that will thresh out the noted discrepancy between
the quantities received and the actual administered vaccines as per
VAS Data;
b) Properly plan and monitor the utilization of COVID-19 vaccines,
including nearly expiring vaccines, to prevent wastage thereof; and
c) Ensure the completeness and correctness of information required
73

in the submission of reports to DOH and other oversight bodies on
the roll out of the Vaccination Program of the National
Government.

11.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Health Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements
of allowances should adhere to the guidelines set forth in DBM-DOH
Joint Circular No. 2022-0001; and, that such claims should be duly
supported with the prescribed documentary requirements, subject to
existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

12.9. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the HRMO to
ensure that seminars/trainings/conventions attended by its officials and
employees is responsive to the organizational result and development
outcomes as embodied in the Capability Development Agenda.

13.7. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive the following courses of
action:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure
that claims of RATA of each official for every month correspond
only to its actual work/attendance, and complete supporting
documents in the payroll pertaining to the DTRs, proof of service or
any equivalent document; and
b) Enjoin the HRMO to monitor and verify the approved Application
for Leave and signed DTRs of LGU officials, and require timely
submission of said documents.

14.4. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and Human Resources and Management Officer to strictly
observe the mandatory monthly minimum take-home pay threshold by
refraining from accommodating salary deductions for private lending
institutions and ensure compliance with the law.

1.10. We further recommended that the last two items in Table 1.2 should be
addressed to by the Municipal Accounting Office immediately in order
to present them fairly in the financial statements.

2.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer /Assistant Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Require the Revenue Collecting Clerks to remit the collection
had by them on a daily basis and to prepare and submit the
corresponding report of collections and deposits thereon.
b) Observe the prescribed period in the remittance of the
collections as provided in Sec 29 of the NGAS Manual in order
to avoid accumulation of cash collections on the hands of the
AOs.
c) Secure the collections in a cash vaults everyday by requiring the
collectors to remit the collections on a daily basis as prescribed.

3.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to identify the breakdown of unlocated difference of
₱21,188,414.98 and to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of Due
to NGAs account by project to regularly monitor the remaining
balances of funds received from NGAs.

4.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile the amounts to be
presented as basis, entity and timing differences in order to prepare a
correct Reconciliation between Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis
as presented in its Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual
Amounts and in the Statement of Financial Performance for the year
then ended, supported with schedules showing how reconciling items
were derived/computed.

5.12 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of
action:
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal
Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of
road component for road projects;
Require the Inventory Committee to submit a Report on the Physical
Count of the Road Network (RPCLRN) and maintain the required
Local Road Inventory and Road Map and Property, Plant and
Equipment-Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components;
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the General Ledger,
Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, Working Paper on the
cost distribution for road components, subsidiary records for road
and road components, depreciation schedule for the depreciable
component of the network system, and prepare the Notes to the
Financial Statements as embodied in COA Circular No. 2015-008
dated November 23, 2015.

6.10 We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to revise the schedule of all PPE
accounts, providing therein the 5% residual value on all items; and
b) Make the necessary adjustments to correct the reported book value
of the PPE and Accumulated Depreciation and Prior Year’s
Adjustment for the Depreciation Expense recorded during the year.

7.15 We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive require:
1. The Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reconcile with the MGSO’s records as regards to the balances
of the Inventory accounts. Moreover, require the MGSO to
submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the required
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued as well as the Report
on Physical Count of Inventories on a semestral basis so that the
data reflected in the books would be updated and corrected to
its accurate balance; and
b. effect the necessary adjustments in the accounting books to
ensure the fair presentation of the Inventory and other affected
accounts in the financial statements.
2. Department/end users to submit the distribution list duly signed
by the identified recipients and be reported in the SSMI for the
Municipal Accounting Office to support the recording of
issuances of these items from stock.
3. Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of Inventory
and reconcile the results thereof with the records maintained in
the Accounting Office.

8.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to correct the noted
deficiencies and submit the same to the audit team for verification.

9.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from approving
for payment the reimbursements made by its officials and employees,
except for travel, emergency purchase, and petty operating expenses.

9.11 Further, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
apply the alternative methods of procurement provided under the 2016
Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184, if public bidding is not feasible.

10.7 We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Accounting
Office to maintain the necessary SLs for a particular controlling account
to facilitate verification of the transactions and in compliance with the
above cited circulars.

10.8 Likewise, update from time to time the SLs and reconcile the same to
the corresponding controlling account in order to rectify errors,
omission, if any, at an earlier date before the financial statements shall
be prepared.

11.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a) The Municipal Budget Officer and the LDRRM Officer to
systematically undertake the preparation of the LDRRMF
Investment Plan, taking into consideration the four thematic areas
where the 70% LDRRMF should be allocated;
b) The LDRRM Officer to fully implement the PPAs in the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan
particularly on disaster preparedness and disaster prevention and
mitigation.

12.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General
Services Officer the following courses of action:
a) Require all drivers of motor vehicles to prepare Trip Ticket for each
authorized travel, properly supply the entries required in the
Driver’s Trip Ticket, and prepare Monthly Report of Official
Travels for each motor vehicle; and
b) Direct the requisitioning office/department to prepare a properly
accomplished Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor
vehicle.

13.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters periodically to enforce
the immediate liquidation/settlement of past due cash advances of all
accountable officers and personnel concerned and enforce strict
compliance with the rules and regulations on cash advances. Require
the active personnel to submit their liquidation documents or refund
the money whichever is applicable under the circumstances.

13.14 Further, require an Accountant’s certification that the previous cash
advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books as part
of the supporting documents. Likewise refrain from granting cash
advances from the national government offices or officers not
connected with the municipality in order to safeguard the government
funds from possible losses or misuse, or else, require them to submit
Certification from the mother office that the purpose of the cash

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries so that Other Land
Improvements and Government Equity account are fairly presented in
the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Coordinate with the respective creditor of the LGU and secure a
document that would warrant the dropping of the amount of
P152,081.71 in the Loans Payable –Domestic account from the books;
b. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reclassify the
P3,316,137.15 in the Due to GOCCs account and record it under the
Loans Payable-Domestic account; and
c. Ensure that the liabilities of the Municipality are properly accounted
for, having in the records a full disclosure of the name of the lending
institution, principal amount and terms of the loan. Likewise, strictly
comply with the requirement to maintain a separate subsidiary ledger
for loans payable, expenditures and interest payments for each
loan/borrowings availed as provided in Section 313 of the Local
Government Code of 1991. This will facilitate detection of unrecorded
loan releases, and principal and interest repayments, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
adjusting journal entries to reflect the remaining balance of the grants in
the Trust Liabilities account under the Special Account of the General
Fund as of December 31, 2020 in order that the account is fairly presented
in the Financial Statements. We also recommended that the Municipal
Accountant maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for the BGCM
account for easy monitoring/tracking of its utilization. Henceforth, strictly
observe Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CY
2020 amounting to ₱4,285,767.00 to the Special Trust Fund and prepare
the necessary adjusting entry so that the Trust Liability-DRRM account is
fairly presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to take up
the receipts from PCA of P4,381,840.00 in the General Fund Books to
reflect the correct balances of the income and payable accounts and to
strictly adhere to the prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts for Local
Government Units provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording
of transactions of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Conduct a regular reconciliation of records with the Real Property
Tax Account Register (RPTAR) of the Municipal Treasurer
pertaining to the set-up of the RPT/SET at the beginning of the
period and consistently adjust these accounts upon their collection;
and
b. Review all entries reflected in the general journal to ensure that all
recorded amounts are correct and complete. Effect the necessary
adjustments if any.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Create a Disposal Committee which shall initiate the process of
disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section
79 of PD 1445 and the rules provided in COA Circular No. 92-386;
b. Require the designated Supply Officer and the Disposal Committee
to prepare the corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the identified unserviceable
properties and; and
c. After the disposal, require the Supply Officer to submit the
necessary documents, together with the official receipt to the Accounting
Office, as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from
the books of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to conduct an inventory of the
Biological Assets, prepare a report of the said assets showing the fair
value of each specie and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant for proper reconciliation; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger
for Biological Assets, update the same based on the report of the
Municipal Agriculturist and ensure that proper accounting and
recognition is made to the Financial Statements as well as to the
Notes to the Financial Statements. Moreover, strictly adhere to the
guidelines set forth under IPSAS 27 in the recognition,
measurement, and disclosure of Biological Assets.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance
Committee (LFC) to submit monthly the Report on Fund Utilization and
Status of Program/Project/Activity to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC and Municipal
Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements as contained in
Appendix E, in compliance to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6,
2020 and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020,
respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
MSWDO/MDRRMO to provide the accounting unit copies of the
RIS/distribution list of relief goods. Further, require the Supply Officer to
ensure the submission of the Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials
Issued (RSMI). These documents and records will serve as proof of validity
and necessity of the purchase of relief goods.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a) MSWDO to cause the immediate submission of the required
documents as prescribed under the rules and procedures of DSWD
Memorandum Circular No. 04 to ensure that the beneficiary of the
social amelioration provisions comply with the documentary
requirements and guarantee attainment of the primary purpose of
the social protection program; and
b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the liquidation documents are
complete and sufficient.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MSWD
Officer to prepare the (1) Summary/List of Donations Received,
Distributed and Balances for In-Kind Donations; (2) Report on the
Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; (3) Report on the Receipt and
Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods; and (4) Report on the
Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated PPE; and furnish the same
to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team
within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA
Circular 2014-002 and 2020-009.

We recommended that the:
a. Local Chief Executive direct the designated Supply Officer to
conduct physical inventory, prepare and submit the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and Report of Physical Count
of Inventories (RPCI), pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual
for LGU; and
b. Strictly comply with Sections 119, 121 and 123 of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs, Volume I on records to be maintained and proper
documentation in the issuances of supplies using the prescribed
forms.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
d. Direct the Supply Officer to conduct pre-repair
evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its
government vehicles subjected to repairs.
e. Require the Supply Officer to prepare and submit the Certificate of
Acceptance and Report of Waste Material and approved by the LCE.
f. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a ledger card for the
expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every
vehicle owned by the LGU to monitor whether the property is
economically repairable.

We reiterated our recommendation that management strictly comply with the required physical count of all properties at least once a year and to submit a copy of the Inventory Report to the audit Team not later than January 31 and every year thereafter. Prepare and maintain updated Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, and conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances of both records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry for the stale checks
of ₱365,607.19 so that the Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts
are fairly presented in the financial statements, and to submit regularly
and promptly the required paid checks and debit/credit memos together
with the bank reconciliation statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable
officials and employees of the LGU with outstanding cash advances to
immediately settle their cash advances by submitting the necessary
documents as evidence of the disbursement made or thru cash refund of
the unused cash advances. As provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 cause
the withholding of salaries and other benefits of employees to settle their
unliquidated cash advances. Likewise, exert extra effort to locate the
whereabouts of those who have retired or out of service, and also issue
demand letters to liquidate their outstanding cash advance.

We recommended the following courses of action:
a. The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the General Service Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road component for road projects;
b. The Municipal General Service Officer/Inventory Committee to
submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network
(RPCLRN) and maintain the required Local Road Inventory and
Road Map and Property, Plant and Equipment-Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
c. The Municipal Accountant to maintain the General Ledger,
Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, Working Paper on the
cost distribution for road components, subsidiary records for road
and road components, depreciation schedule for the depreciable
component of the network system, and prepare the Notes to the
Financial Statements as embodied in COA Circular 2015-008 dated
November 23, 2015 be observed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the records of the subsidiary accounts with the
concerned agencies and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
correct its balance. We further recommend strict compliance with the
rules on presentation of Financial Statements as provided in paragraph
29 (b) and Appendix A, IPSAS I, Volume I of the PPSAS Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CY
2019 amounting to ₱2,814,913.12 to the Special Trust Fund and prepare
the necessary adjusting entry so that the Trust Liability-DRRM account
is fairly presented in the financial statements. Henceforth, strictly
observe Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to
report all benefits and remuneration received by Municipal officials and
employees in the computation of their respective taxable income and
withhold the correct amount of tax to avoid incurring penalties of the
withholding agents of the LGU. Further, we recommend that the
municipal officials and employees concerned to pay the tax due to the
BIR.

We reiterate our prior years’ recommendation that Management: 1. Expedite the
implementation of the programs/projects in order to serve the purpose for which
the funds were released; 2. Submit to the audit team copy/ies of the MOAs for each fund
received that serve as a guide in the implementation of the projects; and 3. Return the
unused fund transfer and unutilized balance of completed projects to the source
agencies in compliance with Section 4.9 and Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-
013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that officials of the LGU adhere to the provisions of
NDRRMC/DBM/DILG JMC Nos. 2014-1 and 2013-1. The MDRRM
Officer should be required to submit the budgetary requirements for the
regular operation of the MDRRM Office. While the allocated LDRRM
Fund shall be exclusively utilized to support disaster risk management
activities that covers the 30% Quick Response Fund and 70% allocation
for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response,
rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and concerned officials
identify and address the causes for the non-implementation of the
projects. Further, we also recommend the immediate implementation of
the projects within the budget year to achieve the desirable socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that the Municipality fully utilize the GAD funds
through the execution of GAD related programs and activities in order to
realize the objective for which the funds were provided.

We reiterate the recommendation that the Solid Waste Management Board
with the direct supervision of the Local Chief Executive to fully implement
the approved 10-year SWMP to attain its objectives.

We recommended that the LGU should immediately initiate action to
enforce settlement of disallowances and suspensions in accordance with
the COA Revised Rules of Procedures on the Settlement of Accounts
(RRSA).

We also recommended that the audit disallowance already issued with
the Notice of Finality of Decision be recorded as a receivable in
compliance with Section 22.6 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer to liquidate all his cash advances within five days after each
fifteen (15) day/end of the month pay period. Henceforth, strictly comply
with the rules set forth in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated Feb. 10, 1997
in the liquidation of cash advances.

1.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements
from July to December, 2018 for all funds which she failed to prepare
the bank reconciliation statement.

1.12 Furthermore, prepare the necessary adjusting entry for the stale checks
of ₱304,283.33 so that the Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts
are fairly presented in the financial statements. Henceforth, strictly
observe Sections 74 and 97 of PD 1445.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to prepare and submit a duly certified list showing the names
of taxpayers and the corresponding amount due and collectible for the
year to the Municipal Accountant which should serve as the basis for
recording the Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax
Receivable at the beginning of the year.

3.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable
officials and employees of the LGU with outstanding cash advances to
immediately settle their cash advances by submitting the necessary
documents as evidence of the disbursement made or thru cash refund of
the unused cash advances. Likewise, exert extra effort to locate the
whereabouts of those who have retired or out of service, and cause the
liquidation of their outstanding cash advance.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer/Supply Officer to prepare and consolidate weekly the
Requisition and Issued Slip using the SSMI as the basis by the Municipal
Accountant to record the procured and issued inventories.

5.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting journal entry to take up the
completed Water Supply System of Ᵽ9,778,095.24 in the General Fund
books to reflect the correct balances of the Property, Plant and
Equipment and Government Equity account.

6.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately remit the taxes withheld on payment for
supplies/materials and services of the Municipality. Henceforth, ensure
that the taxes withheld on payment for supplies/materials and services
are remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue within ten (10) days
following the end of the month the withholding was made or the
withholding accrued.

7.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately remit the collections of the Municipality from
Documentary Stamp Tax. Henceforth, ensure that the collections from
Documentary Stamp Tax are remitted to Bureau of Internal Revenue
within five (5) days after the close of the month.

8.16 We recommended that Management expedite the implementation of the
programs/projects in order to serve the purpose for which the funds
were released and not to deprive of the benefits that may be derived by
the constituents. Also, submit copy/ies of the MOAs for each fund
received that serve as a guide in the implementation of the projects.

8.17 Furthermore, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct
the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to return the
unutilized balance of completed projects to the source agencies in
compliance with Section 4.9 and Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013
dated December 13, 1994.

9.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CY
2018 amounting to ₱2,809,759.41 to the Special Trust Fund, and the
amount of ₱3,096,884.14 for FY 2013 not fully utilized after five (5) years
be reverted back to the general fund and be made available for other
social services to be identified by the local sanggunian in accordance to
Section 21 of RA 10121 and require the Local Budget Officer to
appropriate 5% of the total estimated revenue from regular sources for
LDRRMF.

10.14 We recommended that the Municipality expedite the completion of the
development projects and optimal utilization of the 20% Development
Fund in order to attain the desired socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes as envisioned in DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

11.10 We recommended that the LGU maximize the utilization of the GAD
funds through the implementation of GAD related programs and
projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided.

12.6 We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board with the
direct supervision of the Local Chief Executive strictly adhere the
approved 10-year SWMP for the optimum realization of objectives of
RA No. 9003.

and the amount due and collectible for the year should be prepared by the Municipal
Assessor and submitted to the Municipal Treasurer for recording in the RPTAR and
the Municipal Accountant as the basis for recording the Real Property Tax /Special
Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer conduct intensive tax campaign,
employ collection strategies, and institute legal remedies pursuant to the pertinent
provisions of RA 7160 in order to persuade the Taxpayers to pay their tax on time.

We recommend that management refrain from practicing the system of
procurement thru reimbursements and strictly implement the provisions of the
Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184). Furthermore, we recommend that all
procurement should be supported with complete documentation and the supporting
documents should be properly and completely filled up.

We recommend that management exercise prudence or diligence of a good father of
a family in giving authority to travel that only those that are necessary and responsive
in the exigency of the service are authorized. We further recommend that claims for
travel should be made in pursuance to the provisions of EO 298.

We recommend that management require the Philippine Councilor’s League,
Vice Mayor’s League of the Philippines and League of Municipalities of the Philippines
organized by elected officials, to issue a government official receipt (Accountable Form
No. 51) for all collections made so that proper financial accountability could be
established. Further, ensure that all future payment to these leagues for membership
dues, registration fees and others be receipted with a government official receipt which
is AF No. 51.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to comply strictly with the
reporting guidelines for the DRRM Fund of the LGU pursuant to Sections 5.1.2
and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management workout to have an updated solid waste
management plan pursuant to the provisions of Sections 16 and 17 of RA 9003.
Likewise, we recommend that management strictly enforce the other relevant
provisions of the said law in order to attain a systematic and comprehensive solid waste
management for the protection of public health and the environment.

We recommend that management comply with the provisions of RA 656.
It should insure all its insurable physical assets with the General Insurance Fund of the
GSIS to secure thereof from acts of nature, accidents and man-made calamities and
protect the agency from possible economic setbacks.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and
Accountant to observe strictly Section 347 of RA7160 and Section 7.2.1 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of accounts and financial reports. This
recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of the corresponding Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance on the unsubmitted accounts and/or results of post-audit of
transactions.

We recommend that management implement immediately the unimplemented
projects under the 20% DF and henceforth, implement within the timeframe as
scheduled in the agency Annual Investment Program all development projects,
consistent with the general policy of the government and mandated responsibility of
LGU officials as embodied in the Joint DBM-DILG Memorandum Circular 2011-1, in
order to attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental management
outcomes.

We recommend that management conduct physical inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment and render a report thereon duly categorized by type of property and equipment. We also recommend that the required PPE Ledger Cards be maintained at the accounting unit for purposes of reconciliation, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of C0A Circular No. 92-386 and Section 111 of PD 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU reclassify Fund Transfers, receipts/donation items in the Other Payables account in the Trust Fund with regards to other inactive subsidiaries, appropriate subsidiaries, and the generally accepted accounting principles in order to arrive at the fair presentation of Other Payables and other affected accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: AAR accountant has made a reevaluate the account set up with the mayor on prior years.
observations.reason: needs to reevaluate the account set up during the prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to observe strictly Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, and render accounting reports within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: AAR accountant Finding No.3 duly prepared and submitted the required reports.
observations.reason: Submission of accounts for CY 2015 were submitted for audit however accounts for CY 2016 were still delayed and the number of days delayed for most of the months have increased.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management (a) enforce liquidation of the funds received in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; (b) fast track the implementation of the projects since funds are readily available in order not to deprive the intended beneficiaries of its immediate use; and (c) return to the Source Agency the balance of unutilized funds for projects already completed to enable the national government to reprogram the funds for the other felt-need programs/project.
observations.managementAction: Some of the funds received from the NGAs have already been going. One fund through which submission of liquidation reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; (b) the LGU has been working on the implementation of the projects since funds are readily available in order not to deprive the intended beneficiaries of its immediate use; and (c) returned of the unspent portion of the fund is subject to the conditions stated in the respective Memorandum of Agreements executed between the LGU and the proponent government agencies.
observations.reason: There are other funds which no longer can be implemented and was returned to the source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere strictly with the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, and require the concerned accountable officers to conduct reconciliation of their respective accounts at least quarterly pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR, No. 5 The accountant has readied the respective accountabilities for the immediate liquidation and settlement of cash advances specially those outstanding for more than one year. Demand should be made for the liquidation and settlement of the outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Cash advances of former officials and employees were being paid through salary deductions. Some cash advances paid were not liquidated. Management has not made any effort to locate their whereabouts and demand liquidation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU immediately release/remit the funds to the concerned barangays and/or the provincial government in pursuance to Sec. 271 of RA 7160 so that the latter could utilize the same for their programs and projects. Likewise we recommend that a subsidiary ledger should be maintained for the said account at the Accounting Office in order to provide complete information of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: AAR No.6 The LGU has released the shares of barangays for the current year. Likewise the accountant required to maintain a subsidiary ledger to reflect the complete information of the transactions.
observations.reason: Shares pertaining to prior years from the collection of barangays were not yet remitted to the barangays. Likewise some of the shares were remitted but only for the current year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and require the concerned officials and employees to prepare and submit the necessary documents/reports relative to the receipts and distribution/issuance of donated goods for the DRRM from local and foreign sources.
observations.managementAction: AAR No. 7 Henceforth require the concerned officials and employees to prepare and submit the necessary documents/reports relative to the receipts and distribution/issuance of donated goods for the DRRM from local and foreign sources.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare and submit to the auditor the AAPSI within 60 days from receipt of the audit report copy so that the auditor could evaluate the implementation of the recommendations.
observations.managementAction: AAPSI was submitted the AAPSI within 60 days after receipt of the audit report.
observations.reason: The AAPSI was prepared and submitted but was furnished beyond the prescribed period of 60 days leaving no sufficient time for its evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare and submit a timely and complete Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the 14 bank accounts. She should also be required to work back on prior years' bank statements and disbursements records to identify the reported reconciling items in the BRS labeled as 'unreconciled items' and to record all valid reconciling items prior to the preparation of the year-end FS so that the Cash in Bank account is fairly presented.
observations.managementAction: AAR No.1 The accountant prepared the BRS for all the 14 bank accounts. She has also worked back on prior years bank statements and disbursement records to identify the reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere strictly with the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, and require the concerned accountable officers to conduct reconciliation of their respective accounts at least quarterly pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR No.4 The LGU's concerned accountable officers were required to prepare and submit the reconciliation of their respective accounts. The reconciliations were prepared and submitted for the outstanding balances.
observations.reason: There are reconciling items which remained unreconciled and prior years balances still outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere strictly with the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and the guidelines provided in Local Budget Circular No. 98 on the 45% limitation in appropriation for Personal Services.
observations.managementAction: AAR No.7 The LGU has adjusted and monitored its appropriation and expenditure to comply with the required limitation in appropriation for Personal Services.
observations.reason: There was implementation of corrective measures but prior excesses remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned officials and employees to refund the amounts received for the alleged unauthorized benefit/allowance. Henceforth, refrain from granting similar benefit to individuals in the absence of any legal or statutory basis. Likewise, strictly observe the limitation for the appropriation and expenditures for personal services as provided in Section 325(a) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR No.8 Some personnel concerned have already refunded the amounts disallowed. Management urged to strictly observe the limitation for the appropriation and expenditures for personal services.
observations.reason: Some of the personnel have refunded the amounts but full recovery/settlement is still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board prepare a more detailed annual school board budget which should include priority projects and activities outlined in Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160 and the DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 1998.
observations.managementAction: AAR No.9 The Local School Board prepared and submitted a detailed annual school board budget which includes the required priority programs and projects.
observations.reason: The Local School Board submitted the budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately revert the balance of unutilized PDAF to the unappropriated surplus of the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: AAR No.10 Management has remitted the unutilized PDAF back to the National Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct a physical count of inventories and submit a report thereon to the auditor. Likewise, instruct the Accountant to submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) to support the recorded issuances.
observations.managementAction: AAR No.3 The inventories were physically counted and required supporting documents (SSMI and RIS) were submitted to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned officials to gather all supporting documents required for the immediate registration of the purchased parcels of land with the Registry of Deed and to facilitate the issuance of the certificates of title in favor of the municipality. Henceforth, adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated July 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR No.8 Management required to gather the necessary supporting documents for registration with the Register of Deeds.
observations.reason: No action was made by management during the year to complete supporting documents of the land purchase. No deed of sale registered.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit complete and valid supporting documents to establish the eligibility of beneficiaries and the propriety of the disbursement. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance as a result of post audit of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: AAR No.9 The Notice of Suspension was already served to the concerned beneficiaries. Management to require submission of supporting documents to establish the validity of the transaction.
observations.reason: Documents evidencing the accomplishment report and eligibility of beneficiaries were gathered although further evaluation is required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a Memorandum of Agreement with the Northern Samar Multi-Purpose Cooperative that embodies the relevant terms of reference on the utilization, monitoring and reporting requirements and require the submission of supporting documents to ensure proper use of the funds and effective implementation of the project.
observations.managementAction: The Notice of Suspension was served to NSMPC for the utilization of the funds. Management required the submission of supporting documents and liquidation from the NSMPC.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were gathered but there is difficulty in securing the Memorandum of Agreement from the accounting office because of change of financial management.
